title: Extraction of High Molecular Weight DNA from Aureococcus anophagefferens Virus
author: Alex Truchon

steps.title: Infect 1 L of Aureococcus anophagefferens...
steps.instruction: Infect 1 L of Aureococcus anophagefferens CCMP1984 with 10 mL of Aureococcus anophagefferens Virus (AaV) lysate.

steps.title: Incubate cultures at 19 degrees C...
steps.instruction: Incubate cultures at 19 degrees C on a 14:10 light-dark cycle until complete lysis of the culture (~10 days).

steps.title: Concentrate the lysed culture to a...
steps.instruction: Concentrate the lysed culture to a final volume of 50 – 100 mL final volume via tangential flow filtration using a 30 kDa filter.

steps.title: Centrifuge concentrated lysate for 00:10:00 at...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge concentrated lysate for 00:10:00 at 3,500 xg at 4° C to pellet cellular organisms in lysate.

steps.title: Remove supernatant and discard pellet
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant and discard pellet.

steps.title: Add 10% Triton-X to supernatant to...
steps.instruction: Add 10% Triton-X to supernatant to a final concentration of 1%.

steps.title: Centrifuge 50 mL lysate at a...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 50 mL lysate at a speed of 24,000 xG at 4° C for 01:15:00 to pellet viruses. Discard supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend the pelleted viruses in a...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pelleted viruses in a 500 µL of 50 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.5, 10 mM MgCl2.

steps.title: Add 9 µL DNAse I (2
steps.instruction: Add 9 µL DNAse I (2.0 mg/mL) and mix gently.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 01:00:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 01:00:00 .

steps.title: Add 6 µL of 500 mM EDTA (pH 8
steps.instruction: Add 6 µL of 500 mM EDTA (pH 8.0). Mix gently.

steps.title: While incubating, prepare 100 mL 2%...
steps.instruction: While incubating, prepare 100 mL 2% molecular grade agarose solution by microwaving agarose/water mixture .

steps.title: Add 2% agarose solution to concentrated...
steps.instruction: Add 2% agarose solution to concentrated lysate at a 1:1 ratio for a final concentration of 1% agarose. Store in water bath until ready to cast.

steps.title: Cast agarose—lysate mixture in BioRad CHEF...
steps.instruction: Cast agarose—lysate mixture in BioRad CHEF Mapper Plug Molds (Catalog No. 1703713) and allow to solidify at room temperature.

steps.title: Prepare viral lysis buffer
steps.instruction: Prepare viral lysis buffer.

steps.title: After agarose has solidified punch out...
steps.instruction: After agarose has solidified punch out of molds and add two per Eppendorf tube with 1 mL lysis buffer.

steps.title: Add proteinase-K to a final concentration...
steps.instruction: Add proteinase-K to a final concentration of 1 mg/ml to each tube.

steps.title: Incubate over night at 37° C...
steps.instruction: Incubate over night at 37° C under gentle rotation (~ 2-3 xG or 100 rpm) in a shaking incubator.

steps.title: Cast a 2% low-melting agarose gel...
steps.instruction: Cast a 2% low-melting agarose gel and allow at least 01:00:00 for gel to solidify.

steps.title: Decant lysis buffer from overnight incubations of plugs
steps.instruction: Decant lysis buffer from overnight incubations of plugs.

steps.title: Prepare TNE wash buffer (10 mM...
steps.instruction: Prepare TNE wash buffer (10 mM Tris-Base, 200 mM NaCL, 0.5 mM EDTA). Filter sterilize through a 0.22 µM syringe filter.

steps.title: Wash with 1 mL TNE buffer...
steps.instruction: Wash with 1 mL TNE buffer by resuspending and shaking at room temperature for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Pour off TNE buffer
steps.instruction: Pour off TNE buffer.

steps.title: Repeat steps 22-23 once
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 22-23 once.

steps.title: Carefully insert plugs into individual wells...
steps.instruction: Carefully insert plugs into individual wells of the low melting agarose gel using a sterile razor blade or pipette tip.

steps.title: Seal the wells by pouring liquid...
steps.instruction: Seal the wells by pouring liquid 2% agarose over the wells and allow poured agarose to solidify.

steps.title: Run gel at approximately 120 V...
steps.instruction: Run gel at approximately 120 V for 01:30:00 to 02:00:00 or until a high molecular weight band of DNA can be visualized.

steps.title: Excise high molecular weight bands from...
steps.instruction: Excise high molecular weight bands from the agarose under UV light, being careful to remove as little excess agarose as possible. Place excised pieces of gel in Eppendorf tubes.

steps.title: Melt agarose in a water bath...
steps.instruction: Melt agarose in a water bath set to 80° C Bring volume in tube up to 500 µL with MilliQ water. The melted agarose volume should never exceed 500 µL.

steps.title: Once entirely melted (after at least...
steps.instruction: Once entirely melted (after at least ten minutes), add 1 mL basic phenol to the tube and invert to mix.

steps.title: Place phenol—agarose mixture at -80° C for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Place phenol—agarose mixture at -80° C for 00:10:00

steps.title: Centrifuge the mixture for 00:05:00 at...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the mixture for 00:05:00 at maximum speed on a microcentrifuge at room temperature.

steps.title: Carefully remove upper aqueous layer and...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove upper aqueous layer and place in a clean 2 mL Eppendorf tube. Discard the lower phenol layer.

steps.title: Add 500 µL phenol and 500...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL phenol and 500 µL chloroform to the aqueous layer. Centrifuge at max speed for 00:05:00 at room temperature. Pipette aqueous layer into a new 2 mL Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Repeat step 33-34 until there is...
steps.instruction: Repeat step 33-34 until there is no remaining protein debris between the aqueous and the phenol layer.

steps.title: Add 1 mL chloroform to the aqueous layer
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL chloroform to the aqueous layer. Centrifuge at full speed for 00:05:00 . Pipette aqueous layer into a clean 2 mL Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Repeat step 36 once
steps.instruction: Repeat step 36 once.

steps.title: Precipitate the DNA by adding 3...
steps.instruction: Precipitate the DNA by adding 3 M sodium acetate to a final concentration of 0.1 M (~67 µL in 2 mL) and fill to 2 mL with 100% EtOH.

steps.title: Precipitate DNA at -80℃ for at least 02:00:00 or overnight
steps.instruction: Precipitate DNA at -80℃ for at least 02:00:00 or overnight.

steps.title: Centrifuge samples at maximum speed at 4° C for 01:00:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge samples at maximum speed at 4° C for 01:00:00 .

steps.title: Remove supernatant and resuspend DNA pellet...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant and resuspend DNA pellet in 1 mL 70% EtOH.

steps.title: Centrifuge samples at maximum speed at 4° C for 01:00:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge samples at maximum speed at 4° C for 01:00:00 .

steps.title: Remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant. Allow residual EtOH to evaporate on a heat block set to 37° C.

steps.title: Resuspend DNA pellet in MilliQ water
steps.instruction: Resuspend DNA pellet in MilliQ water.

steps.title: Do not allow to solidify
steps.instruction: Do not allow to solidify: hold in a water bath set to 50° C.

steps.title: Add 750 µL 20% SDS, 500 µL 0
steps.instruction: Add 750 µL 20% SDS, 500 µL 0.5 M EDTA, and 200 µL 3 M sodium acetate to a conical tube.

steps.title: Bring to 50 mL with MilliQ water
steps.instruction: Bring to 50 mL with MilliQ water.

steps.title: Filter sterilize through a 0
steps.instruction: Filter sterilize through a 0.22 µM syringe filter.

steps.title: If phenol-agarose has frozen, allow sample to thaw before centrifugation
steps.instruction: If phenol-agarose has frozen, allow sample to thaw before centrifugation.

steps.title: Quantify extracted DNA on Nanodrop or...
steps.instruction: Quantify extracted DNA on Nanodrop or Qubit per manufacturer’s specifications before sequencing.
